system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   28.96;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          1004.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volVectorField;
    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (1.3 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (1.3 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (1.3 0 0);
        value           uniform (1.3 0 0);
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    obstacle
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    defaultFaces
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    obstacle
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    defaultFaces
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    obstacle
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    defaultFaces
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         4;

deltaT          0.002;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.1;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.5;

maxDeltaT       1;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
    reconstruct(p)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Left leg (x=0 to x=0.6)
    (0    0    -0.05)   // 0
    (0.6  0    -0.05)   // 1
    (0.6  0.2  -0.05)   // 2
    (0    0.2  -0.05)   // 3
    (0    0     0.05)   // 4
    (0.6  0     0.05)   // 5
    (0.6  0.2   0.05)   // 6
    (0    0.2   0.05)   // 7

    // Step area (x=0.6 to x=3)
    (3    0    -0.05)   // 8
    (3    0.2  -0.05)   // 9
    (3    1    -0.05)   // 10
    (0.6  1    -0.05)   // 11
    (3    0.2   0.05)   // 12
    (3    1     0.05)   // 13
    (0.6  1     0.05)   // 14
    (0    1    -0.05)   // 15
    (0    1     0.05)   // 16

    // Right leg (x=3)
    (3    1     0.05)   // 17 (same as 13, used for completeness)
    (3    0.2   0.05)   // 18 (same as 12)
    (3    0    -0.05)   // 19 (same as 8)
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: left leg
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (25 8 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: step area
    hex (1 8 9 2 5 12 6 13) (100 33 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: right leg
    hex (11 14 10 15 14 13 17 16) (100 42 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
            (15 16 7 3)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (19 12 13 18)
        );
    }
    bottom
    {
        type symmetryPlane;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
            (1 8 12 5)
            (11 14 13 10)
        );
    }
    top
    {
        type symmetryPlane;
        faces
        (
            (15 16 14 11)
            (10 13 14 11)
        );
    }
    obstacle
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (2 3 7 6)
            (1 2 6 5)
            (8 9 12 5)
            (9 10 13 12)
            (1 5 12 8)
            (2 6 13 9)
            (11 14 13 10)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>
<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.rhoCentralFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh" in times "0" down to constant

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (11 14 10 15 14 13 17 16) (100 42 1) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(11 14 16 15) 4(14 10 17 13) 

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run2/runs/forwardStep/3/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 50.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

Looking at the current error logs:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: Block hex `(11 14 10 15 14 13 17 16)` has "inward-pointing faces". Specifically, faces `4(11 14 16 15)` and `4(14 10 17 13)` are inward-pointing. This means the vertex ordering in this hex block produces a negative volume, making the block invalid.

2. **`log.rhoCentralFoam`**: Cannot find the `points` file, which is a cascading error from blockMesh failing — no mesh was generated.

The root cause is the **incorrect block topology**. Let me carefully analyze the block definition:

```
hex (11 14 10 15 14 13 17 16)
```

This block reuses vertices 14 and 13 from the previous block, but vertices 16 and 17 are duplicates of vertices 7 and 14 respectively. More importantly, the vertex ordering creates a degenerate/inverted hexahedron. The face `(11 14 16 15)` uses vertices that don't form a proper planar face given the spatial arrangement.

Additionally, looking at the vertex coordinates:
- Vertex 11: `(0.6, 1, -0.05)`
- Vertex 14: `(0.6, 1, 0.05)`
- Vertex 10: `(3, 1, -0.05)`
- Vertex 15: `(0, 1, -0.05)`
- Vertex 13: `(3, 1, 0.05)`
- Vertex 17: `(3, 1, 0.05)` (duplicate of 13)
- Vertex 16: `(0, 1, 0.05)` (duplicate of 7)

All these vertices lie on y=1. This entire "right leg" region is collinear in the y-direction — it's essentially a flat plane, not a volumetric block. The geometry needs to be reconsidered.

## Resolution

The mesh design needs to be corrected. The forwardStep tutorial typically uses a stepped geometry where the step extends upward. The current vertex layout has all right-side vertices at y=0, y=0.2, y=1 along x=0.6 to x=3, but the block topology is wrong.

Here's the corrected approach for the blockMeshDict:

1. **Fix Block 2**: The hex `(11 14 10 15 14 13 17 16)` is geometrically invalid. It should represent the upper-right region above the step. Replace it with the correct block definition. Based on the standard forwardStep tutorial geometry, the right leg should span from y=0 to y=1 at x=3. The corrected block should be:

   ```
   // Block 2: right leg (corrected)
   hex (11 14 10 15 14 13 17 16) → replace with:
   hex (11 14 10 15 14 13 17 16) ... wait, let me redefine properly.
   ```

   Actually, looking at the vertex coordinates more carefully, the issue is that vertices 11=(0.6,1,-0.05), 14=(0.6,1,0.05), 10=(3,1,-0.05), 15=(0,1,-0.05), 13=(3,1,0.05), 16=(0,1,0.05) are ALL at y=1. This means the "right leg" block would have zero thickness in y — it's degenerate.

   **The fundamental fix**: Redesign the vertex layout so that the right leg spans from y=0 to y=1. Change vertex 15 from `(0, 1, -0.05)` to `(0, 1, -0.05)` and vertex 16 from `(0, 1, 0.05)`. Then define Block 2 correctly as:

   ```
   // Block 2: right leg (upper region above step level)
   hex (11 14 10 15 14 13 17 16)
   ```

   But the vertex ordering must be correct. The standard hex ordering is (bottom-left-front, bottom-right-front, bottom-right-back, bottom-left-back, top-left-front, top-right-front, top-right-back, top-left-back). For Block 2, it should be:

   ```
   hex (15 16 14 11 10 13 17 14)
   ```

   Wait — this is messy because vertices 14 and 13 are at (0.6,1) and (3,1), sharing y-coordinates with 11 and 15. The real problem is that the geometry as drawn has the step extending from x=0.6 to x=3 at y=0.2, and the right leg goes from y=0 to y=1 at x=3. The block between x=0.6→3, y=0.2→1 is missing.

   **Recommended fix**: Add the missing vertices and blocks. Add vertices at (0.6, 0, -0.05)=17, (0.6, 0, 0.05)=18, (3, 0, -0.05)=19, (3, 0, 0.05)=20, (3, 0.2, -0.05)=21, (3, 0.2, 0.05)=22, (0, 0, -0.05)=23, (0, 0, 0.05)=24, (0, 0.2, -0.05)=25, (0, 0.2, 0.05)=26. Then define three proper blocks: left leg (0→0.6, 0→0.2), step area (0.6→3, 0→0.2), and right leg (3, 0→0.2).**

2. **Simpler alternative**: Reduce to a minimal valid mesh with just two blocks (left leg and step area), removing the right leg entirely since it adds no flow-developing region. Define only:
   - Block 0: left leg `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)`
   - Block 1: step area `hex (1 8 9 2 5 12 6 13)`
   
   And update all boundary face definitions accordingly, removing references to the right leg and unused vertices.

Given that Attempt 4 suggested adding a full right leg but the current geometry has all right-leg vertices coplanar at y=1, the simplest fix is to **remove the right leg block entirely** and only keep Blocks 0 and 1. Update the boundary definitions to remove any faces referencing vertices 11-16. This eliminates the degenerate block causing the inward-pointing faces error.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>do a laminar, compressible flow over a forward-facing step using the rhoCentralFoam solver. Boundary conditions include a fixed velocity of 1.3m/s and temperature of 1 K at the inlet and slip conditions on the obstacle. Use timestep of 0.002 and output every 0.1. Finaltime is 4.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.

Add vertices: 17=(0.6 0 -0.05), 18=(0.6 0 0.05), 19=(3 0 -0.05), 20=(3 0 0.05), 21=(3 0.2 -0.05), 22=(3 0.2 0.05), 23=(0 0 -0.05), 24=(0 0 0.05), 25=(0 0.2 -0.05), 26=(0 0.2 0.05). Replace all blocks with: Block 0 left leg hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (25 8 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1), Block 1 step area hex (1 8 9 2 5 12 6 13) (100 33 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1), Block 2 lower right leg hex (11 19 20 14 12 21 13 15) (100 33 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1), Block 3 upper right leg hex (19 8 17 14 21 22 20 15) (100 33 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1), Block 4 right wall hex (8 9 21 22 12 10 13 15) (100 42 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1). Update boundary: inlet faces from (0 4 7 3) only, outlet faces from (19 12 13 20), bottom faces from (0 1 5 4) and (1 8 12 5) and (19 8 17 14) and (21 22 20 15), top faces from (15 16 14 11) and (10 13 14 11) and (8 9 21 22) and (21 10 19 15), obstacle faces from existing ones plus new ones: (2 3 7 6), (1 2 6 5), (8 9 12 5), (9 10 13 12), (1 5 12 8), (2 6 13 9), (19 8 17 14), (21 19 20 15), (8 17 18 9), (9 18 20 12), (11 14 15 10), (11 15 16 14), (19 14 15 20), (8 14 15 17), (11 14 19 15), (19 15 16 20), (8 17 16 14), (11 17 16 14), (11 19 16 15), (8 19 16 17), (11 21 22 10), (10 22 20 11), (19 21 22 20), (19 22 20 19), (8 21 22 17), (11 21 22 17), (19 21 22 17), (8 22 20 17)'), PlannedFileChange(file='system/controlDict', changes='No changes needed, keep as is'), PlannedFileChange(file='system/fvSchemes', changes='No changes needed, keep as is')]
